quiz.title: Geometry: Special Right Triangles — 30°-60°-90°
quiz.subtitle: Unit 8 Quiz 3: Ratios of Side Lengths in a Scalene Right Triangle (10 Questions)
quiz.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: In a 30°-60°-90° triangle, which side is the shortest?
options: The side opposite the square corner
questions.explanation: In any triangle, the shortest side is opposite the smallest angle. In a 30°-60°-90° triangle, the smallest angle is 30°, so the side opposite it is the shortest.

questions.question: In a 30°-60°-90° triangle, if the shortest side (opposite the 30° angle) has length $x$, what is the length of the longest side?
questions.explanation: In a 30°-60°-90° triangle, the longest side (opposite the square corner) is always twice the shortest side. If the shortest side is $x$, the longest side is $2x$.

questions.question: In a 30°-60°-90° triangle, if the shortest side (opposite the 30° angle) has length $x$, what is the length of the middle side (opposite the 60° angle)?
questions.explanation: In a 30°-60°-90° triangle, the side opposite the 60° angle is $\sqrt{3}$ times the shortest side. If the shortest side is $x$, the middle side is $x\sqrt{3}$.

questions.question: Which of the following represents the ratio of the sides in a 30°-60°-90° triangle, from shortest to longest?
questions.explanation: The side ratio in a 30°-60°-90° triangle is $1 : \sqrt{3} : 2$. The shortest side (opposite 30°) is $1$, the middle side (opposite 60°) is $\sqrt{3}$, and the longest side (opposite the square corner) is $2$.

questions.question: A 30°-60°-90° triangle has a shortest side of length $6$. What is the length of the longest side?
questions.explanation: The longest side is twice the shortest side: $2 \cdot 6 = 12$.

questions.question: A 30°-60°-90° triangle has a shortest side of length $4$. What is the length of the middle side?
questions.explanation: The middle side (opposite 60°) is the shortest side times $\sqrt{3}$: $4\sqrt{3}$.

questions.question: A 30°-60°-90° triangle has a longest side of length $10$. What is the length of the shortest side?
questions.explanation: The longest side is twice the shortest side: $2x = 10$, so $x = 5$. The shortest side is $5$.

questions.question: A 30°-60°-90° triangle has a longest side of length $14$. What is the length of the middle side?
questions.explanation: First find the shortest side: $2x = 14$, so $x = 7$. Then the middle side is $7\sqrt{3}$.

questions.question: A 30°-60°-90° triangle has a middle side (opposite the 60° angle) of length $5\sqrt{3}$. What is the length of the shortest side?
questions.explanation: The middle side equals the shortest side times $\sqrt{3}$: $x\sqrt{3} = 5\sqrt{3}$, so $x = 5$. The shortest side is $5$.

questions.question: A 30°-60°-90° triangle has a middle side (opposite the 60° angle) of length $9\sqrt{3}$. What is the length of the longest side?
questions.explanation: The middle side is $x\sqrt{3} = 9\sqrt{3}$, so the shortest side $x = 9$. The longest side is twice the shortest: $2 \cdot 9 = 18$.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You have mastered the 30°-60°-90° triangle side relationships. You can find any side given any other side with confidence.
scoreMessages.70: Good work! Remember the ratio $1 : \sqrt{3} : 2$. The shortest side is opposite 30°, the middle side is opposite 60°, and the longest side is opposite the square corner. The longest side is always twice the shortest.
scoreMessages.50: A fair start. Key patterns: (1) shortest side $= x$ (opposite 30°), (2) middle side $= x\sqrt{3}$ (opposite 60°), (3) longest side $= 2x$ (opposite square corner). Always find the shortest side first if you're given the middle or longest side.
scoreMessages.0: Keep practicing! The 30°-60°-90° triangle comes from splitting an equilateral triangle in half along its height. The side ratio is $1 : \sqrt{3} : 2$. The longest side is always twice the shortest side.
